title: Rich heel balm
subtitle: A beeswax, shea, and coconut balm with peppermint for dry and cracked heels
excerpt: Shea butter and coconut oil soften thickened skin; beeswax seals the moisture in and gives the balm enough body to stay on the heel. Peppermint essential oil adds a cooling sensation and has antifungal properties. Apply thickly at night and put on cotton socks.
sourceNotes: Shea butter emollient properties: Akihisa et al., Journal of Oleo Science (2010). Peppermint oil cooling and antifungal activity: Hammer et al., Journal of Applied Microbiology (1999). Occlusive therapy for xerosis: standard dermatology reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale up proportionally; this is a simple ratio of wax to soft oils.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The balm keeps for a year. Make a batch at the start of autumn when dry skin typically worsens.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within one year at room temperature. Discard if the balm develops a rancid smell or a grainy, greasy surface texture.

glossaryTerms.definition: Solid fat from the shea tree seed. Melts at body temperature. Exceptionally emollient -- one of the most effective plant-derived softeners for thickened or dry skin.

content.text: The key to an effective heel balm is an occlusive base that stays on the skin long enough for the moisturising ingredients to penetrate.

provides intense emolliency; coconut oil adds antibacterial properties; beeswax gives the balm enough body to stay on the heel rather than slipping off. The routine is simple: apply at night, put on cotton socks, remove in the morning.

items.prepNote: about 0.75 ml; do not use undiluted on broken skin

content.text: Melt the beeswax pellets in a double boiler until fully liquid. Add the shea butter and coconut oil and stir until melted and combined. Remove from the heat.
Allow the mixture to cool to about 50 to 55°C before adding the peppermint essential oil. Adding it while too hot causes the volatile oils to evaporate before the balm sets.
Pour into a salve tin or small wide-mouth jar. Leave to set undisturbed at room temperature for at least 30 minutes. The balm will be very firm when fully set.

Apply a generous amount to clean, dry heels at night. Work into the heel and the deepest cracks. Put on a pair of clean cotton socks and sleep in them. Remove in the morning and wash with soap and water. Repeat nightly until the cracks have healed, then use two to three times a week as maintenance. The peppermint will feel strongly cooling on the first application.

Keep in the sealed tin at room temperature. Use within one year. Discard if the balm develops a rancid smell. The balm is solid in cool weather and may soften in summer heat -- keep it in a cool cupboard if this is a concern.
